title: Introduction to Arabic Verbs: Dhahab (to go) and Kana (to be)
summary: The session covers the verb forms of dhahab (to go) for plural subjects, introduces kana as a past tense marker, and distinguishes between male and female verb endings in conjugations. Students practice sentence construction using these verbs.
key_points: Dhahab conjugation for plural subjects: dhahabtum (you all went), dhahabnaa (we went).
Kana is used to indicate past tense: e.g., kana al-mudir fi ghurfati (the principal was in his room).
Differentiating between male and female verb endings: qara'tum vs. qara'tunna, sharibtum vs. sharibtunna.
questions: [00:01:00] Q: Is Bilal in the hospital? — A: Yes, may Allah cure him.
[00:21:00] Q: Why do we use amr verbs here? — A: It's a command form used for giving instructions.
